bikes.title: Carbon Frame Bike
bikes.author: prefrontal cortex
randomFacts: The underlying project was shared as an open mixed-reality / digital-twin reference (glTF sample)—handy if you care about 3D pipelines, not just the look of the render.
Carbon fiber frames are built from layered sheets in a mold; the layup pattern drives stiffness, comfort, and weight more than the silhouette alone.
Replace these bullets in JSON with your own trivia: pro palmares, design trivia, or why you picked this model for the gallery.

partsGuide.name: Frame
partsGuide.purpose: Main structure that holds wheels and components; determines fit, stiffness, and ride feel.

partsGuide.name: Fork
partsGuide.purpose: Holds the front wheel and steers the bike; on MTBs often a suspension fork for bump absorption.

partsGuide.name: Wheels (rim + spokes + hub)
partsGuide.purpose: Roll on tires; hubs contain bearings; drivetrain connects at the rear hub.

partsGuide.name: Tires
partsGuide.purpose: Grip and cushion; tread and width suit road, gravel, or trail.

partsGuide.name: Handlebar & stem
partsGuide.purpose: Steering control and cockpit position; stem sets reach and height to the bar.

partsGuide.name: Brakes
partsGuide.purpose: Slow and stop—disc calipers squeeze rotors or rim brakes contact the wheel rim.

partsGuide.name: Crankset & pedals
partsGuide.purpose: Convert leg power into rotation; chainrings define gearing with the cassette.

partsGuide.name: Chain & cassette
partsGuide.purpose: Transfers power to the rear wheel; cassette cogs provide gear range.

partsGuide.name: Derailleurs & shifters
partsGuide.purpose: Move the chain across chainrings (front) and cassette (rear) for easier or harder gears.

partsGuide.name: Seatpost & saddle
partsGuide.purpose: Support rider weight and set saddle height/fore-aft for pedaling comfort.

bikes.title: Mountain Bike
bikes.author: Studio Lab
randomFacts: The modern MTB drivetrain trend toward a single chainring (“1x”) trades front shifts for a wide-range cassette and a quieter, simpler cockpit.
Knobs and casing construction matter as much as tread pattern: small knobs roll faster on hardpack; tall spaced knobs bite in loose dirt.
Dropper posts became standard on trail bikes because being able to lower the saddle beats hanging off the back on steep descents.
Paste a YouTube URL in JSON when you find a great trail-tech explainer for this slot.

partsGuide.name: Suspension fork
partsGuide.purpose: Absorbs front impacts to maintain traction and control on rough terrain.

partsGuide.name: Rear shock (full-suspension)
partsGuide.purpose: Lets the rear wheel track bumps; linkage designs vary by bike category.

partsGuide.name: Dropper post
partsGuide.purpose: Lowers saddle on demand for steep descents; raises for efficient climbing.

partsGuide.name: Wide handlebar
partsGuide.purpose: More leverage for steering precision and stability at speed off-road.

partsGuide.name: Disc brakes
partsGuide.purpose: Consistent stopping in mud and wet; rotors dissipate heat on long descents.

partsGuide.name: Knobby tires
partsGuide.purpose: Tread digs into loose surfaces; lower pressures increase grip on trails.

partsGuide.name: 1x drivetrain
partsGuide.purpose: Single front chainring with wide-range cassette—simple shifting on technical terrain.

bikes.title: Bike Frame (annotated on Sketchfab)
bikes.author: hutcheson3D
randomFacts: This Sketchfab scene is built for learning: the author added annotations for tubes you would measure on a real frame drawing.
Head-tube length and angle interact with fork offset to define how the bike handles at low and high speed.
Seat-tube angle changes where your knee sits over the pedal—small degrees matter for long rides and knee comfort.

partsGuide.name: Head tube
partsGuide.purpose: Houses the steerer; defines stack and houses headset bearings for steering.

partsGuide.name: Top tube / down tube
partsGuide.purpose: Connect head tube to seat cluster and bottom bracket; major structural triangle members.

partsGuide.name: Seat tube
partsGuide.purpose: Holds the seatpost; angle influences saddle position relative to pedals.

partsGuide.name: Chainstays & seatstays
partsGuide.purpose: Rear triangle members connecting bottom bracket and seat cluster to the rear axle.

partsGuide.name: Bottom bracket shell
partsGuide.purpose: Interface for crank bearings; width and standard must match your crankset.

bikes.title: Trek Madone SLR 9 Disc eTap
bikes.author: Dav1d-
randomFacts: The Madone line is Trek’s aero road platform—deep airfoil tube shaping trades a little weight for speed in the wind.
Disc brakes on aero bikes took years to normalize in the pro peloton; they’re now default for wet-weather confidence and rim heat isn’t an issue.
Integrated cockpits look clean and reduce drag, but they can complicate travel and stem swaps—something to know before you dream up your build.
Edit this list with team-bike stories or model-year notes that matter to you.

bikes.title: COLNAGO Y1Rs rainbow Tadej Pogacar edition
bikes.author: Dav1d-
randomFacts: The Y1Rs is Colnago’s aero-focused race platform—think low drag and integrated front ends rather than classic round tubes.
Tadej Pogačar has helped define the modern GC rider who wins climbs, time trials, and one-day races; his bike choices get dissected every season.
Rainbow accents usually nod to a world champion’s special paint—great gallery fodder even when the 3D file is fan art, not a team-issue frame.
Swap in your own lines here (e.g. a specific Monument, Grand Tour stage, or component choice) to match how you want to tell the story.

bikes.title: Scott Plasma S
bikes.author: Dav1d-
randomFacts: Time trial and triathlon bikes chase the lowest sustainable drag: tube shaping, hidden brakes, and deep wheels are all part of the package.
Scott’s Plasma family has spanned everything from entry carbon TT rigs to flagship WorldTour time trial machines.
Fit matters more than frame “speed”: if you can’t hold the position for your event length, the wind tunnel numbers don’t translate.
Add a youtubeUrl when you have a solid Plasma walkthrough or race clip you like.
